name: Prostate Disease (Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia)
description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) is the most common prostatic disease in intact male dogs, caused by age-related hormonal changes. The enlarged prostate compresses the urethra and rectum, causing difficulty urinating and defecating.
treatment_overview: Castration is the simplest and most effective treatment, causing prostate shrinkage within weeks. Medical treatment with osaterone acetate or finasteride is an alternative in dogs intended for breeding.
prevention_tips: Regular prostatic assessment in intact males over 5 years
